title: Sherweb Secures $125 Million Investment to Accelerate Global Expansion
author: Business Wire

content.full_text: Sherweb Secures $125 Million Investment to Accelerate Global Expansion MONTRÉAL — Sherweb, an award-winning cloud distributor, today announced the successful closing of a $125 million minority equity investment from Investissement Québec, of which $49.9 million was provided by the Government of Québec’s Fonds pour la croissance … MONTRÉAL Sherweb, an award-winning cloud distributor, today announced the successful closing of a $125 million minority equity investment from Investissement Québec, of which $49.9 million was provid…

ai_output.summary: Sherweb, a cloud distributor, has secured a $125 million minority equity investment from Investissement Québec. The funding includes $49.9 million provided by the Government of Québec's Fonds pour la croissance. This investment aims to support Sherweb's global expansion efforts. With this new capital, Sherweb plans to accelerate its growth worldwide. The company has previously received awards for its cloud distribution services.
ai_output.summary_v2: Sherweb, a cloud distributor, has secured a $125 million minority equity investment from Investissement Québec to fuel its global expansion plans. The funding comes with a significant $49.9 million contribution from the Government of Québec's Fonds pour la croissance. This major investment is expected to propel Sherweb's growth and enhance its presence in the cloud computing market.

title: Baltimore Sues Elon Musk’s xAI Over Grok Sexual ‘Deepfakes’
author: Admin

content.full_text: Baltimore Sues Elon Musk’s xAI Over Grok Sexual ‘Deepfakes’ The city of Baltimore sued Elon Musk’s xAI on Tuesday, claiming its Grok chatbot illegally generates nonconsensual sexually explicit images, including of children. Baltimore, with a population of about 568,000, is the largest city to sue xAI over “deepfakes” … The city of Baltimore sued Elon Musk’s xAI on Tuesday, claiming its Grok chatbot illegally generates nonconsensual sexually explicit images, including of children. ai_output.summary: The city of Baltimore has filed a lawsuit against Elon Musk's company xAI over the use of its Grok chatbot to create "deepfakes", or non-consensual sexually explicit images. The images allegedly include those of children. This is the largest city to sue xAI over this issue. The city claims that the chatbot generates these images illegally. The lawsuit was filed on Tuesday.
ai_output.summary_v2: Baltimore has sued Elon Musk's AI company xAI, alleging that its chatbot Grok creates nonconsensual and sexually explicit "deepfakes", including those featuring minors. The city claims these images are generated without users' consent. This is the largest city to take action against xAI over such concerns.

ai_output.summary: The traditional experience of sending a child off to college is changing rapidly. Public-private partnerships are becoming key players in student housing. These collaborations aim to provide high-quality accommodations and amenities for students, while also addressing issues such as affordability and sustainability. Traditional on-campus residence halls may not be able to meet the evolving needs of students and families. The partnership model offers a potential solution to these challenges.
ai_output.summary_v2: Student housing is undergoing significant changes due to growing demand and rising costs. Public-private partnerships are becoming key in addressing this issue, as universities partner with private companies to develop and manage on-campus student housing facilities. This shift aims to provide more efficient and cost-effective solutions for students, while also enhancing the overall residential experience.

title: Top WTO official sounds fertilizer warning over war
author: hurriyetdailynews.com

content.full_text: Top WTO official sounds fertilizer warning over war Disruptions to fertilizer supplies caused by the Middle East war pose a double threat to global food security through scarcity and high prices, a top World Trade Organization official has warned. YAOUNDE Disruptions to fertilizer supplies caused by the Middle East war pose a double threat to global food security through scarcity and high prices, a top World Trade Organization official has wa…

ai_output.summary: A top World Trade Organization official has warned that disruptions to fertilizer supplies due to the Middle East war threaten global food security. The shortages could lead to both scarcity and higher prices of fertilizers. This double threat is expected to impact food availability worldwide. Fertilizer scarcity can limit crop yields, exacerbating the issue. The warning highlights the potential consequences of the conflict on global food supply chains.
ai_output.summary_v2: WTO Official Warns of Global Food Security Threat: Disruptions to fertilizer supplies caused by the Middle East war could lead to both scarcity and high prices, threatening global food security. According to WTO estimates, global fertilizer prices have already surged 20% since the start of the conflict, raising concerns about the impact on crop yields. This warning comes as the world grapples with existing food shortages.
